2606.22352.title: On the Sparsity-Storage-Accuracy Tradeoff in Parsimoniously Activated Dictionary Learning
2606.22352.paper_summary: Dictionary learning has long been studied from both optimization and probabilistic perspectives. While formulations with element-wise sparsity regularization (e.g., L1-based sparse coding) admit well-established probabilistic interpretations, many structured variants that impose global constraints lack a clear and tractable generative view. In this paper, we revisit a class of practically effective yet theoretically under-explored dictionary learning methods that impose a simple global regularization on the number of activated dictionary atoms, which we term parsimoniously activated dictionary learning (PADL). We show that PADL admits an equivalent formulation as maximum a posteriori estimation under a structured generative model, with auxiliary latent variables that govern global activation patterns. This formulation allows us to derive generalization guarantees that are difficult to obtain under the original formulation. More importantly, it yields an analytical characterization of the tradeoff between sparsity, storage cost, and reconstruction accuracy, enabling data-driven estimation of optimal hyperparameters. Based on this connection, we develop an efficient and interpretable PADL algorithm that eliminates manual hyperparameter tuning, achieving improved reconstruction performance under comparable sparsity levels on visual benchmarks. We further demonstrate its practical utility in accelerating inference for vision-language models.
2606.22352.paper_summary_zh: 字典學習長期以來從優化和概率的角度進行研究。雖然帶有元素稀疏正則化的公式（例如，基於 L1 的稀疏編碼）具有成熟的概率解釋，但許多施加全局約束的結構變體缺乏明確且可處理的生成視角。在本文中，我們重新探討了一類在實際上有效但在理論上未被充分探索的字典學習方法，這些方法對激活的字典原子數施加了一個簡單的全局正則化，我們稱之為節約激活字典學習（PADL）。我們展示了 PADL 可以作為結構生成模型下的最大後驗估計的等價公式，並引入輔助潛在變量來控制全局激活模式。這一公式使我們能夠推導出在原始公式下難以獲得的泛化保證。更重要的是，它提供了稀疏性、存儲成本和重建準確性之間權衡的分析特徵，從而使得基於數據的最佳超參數估計成為可能。基於這一聯繫，我們開發了一種高效且可解釋的 PADL 算法，消除了手動超參數調整，在可比的稀疏水平下實現了改進的重建性能，並在視覺基準上取得了更好的效果。我們進一步展示了其在加速視覺-語言模型推理中的實用性。
2606.22352.author: Zihui Zhao et.al.
2606.22352.authors: Zihui Zhao, Yuanbo Tang, Yang Li
